cues.title: The three target windows
lines: pH — 5.8 to 6.2 sweet spot, 5.5 to 6.8 acceptable
EC — 850 to 1200 microsiemens vegetative, 1200+ for radish bulbs
Reservoir temperature — 18 to 22 degrees optimum

cues.title: The drift rule
lines: Healthy plants eating nitrate → pH goes up
The cure is acid, almost never base
If pH falls on its own → root rot, not chemistry

cues.title: The carbonate buffer chain
lines: CO₂ + H₂O ⇌ H₂CO₃ ⇌ H⁺ + HCO₃⁻
This pair sits at pH 5 to 7 — exactly your hydroponic range
Acid added → bicarbonate eats it → barely a pH change

cues.caption: Colombo Aquatest full row on shelf — KH, GH, Nitrite, pH, Ammonia, Nitrate, Phosphate variants
Colombo Aquatest KH and GH boxes — €9.99 each

cues.title: Your starter kit at Ranzijn
lines: Colombo Aquatest pH — 9 euros 99
Colombo Aquatest KH — 9 euros 99
Total — 20 euros, lifetime supply for a single tower

cues.title: KH benchmarks
lines: Rainwater — 0 to 1 °dKH (no buffer, easy to overshoot)
Target for hydroponics — 2 to 4 °dKH (gentle buffer)
Almere tap — 8 to 11 °dKH (strong buffer)
Aachen tap — 12 to 18 °dKH (very hard)
Demi / RO water — 0 °dKH

cues.caption: Colombo Quick Test 6-in-1 Teststrip — 50 strips €15.99, multi-parameter quick check
JBL ProAquatest Easy 7-in-1 — tube with color chart for pH, KH, GH, NO2, NO3, Cl2, CO2
SuperFish pH Min — 1L pond/aquarium pH down, €14.50
SuperFish pH Min back panel — Dutch dosing instructions

cues.title: Two stock solutions
lines: Tap water reservoir — 5 grams citric acid in 100 mL water
Rainwater reservoir — 0.5 grams in 100 mL water (10 times more dilute)

cues.title: Dosing protocol
lines: Add 1 mL of stock at a time
Stir, wait 5 minutes for mixing
Re-test pH with the drop kit
Repeat until in target range

cues.caption: Colombo FloraGrow Pro — 500ml aquarium plant fertilizer, €19.95

cues.title: Cold water — more dissolved oxygen
lines: At 12 degrees — 10.8 milligrams per liter at saturation
At 22 degrees — 8.7 milligrams per liter
At 28 degrees — 7.8 milligrams per liter
Roots breathe through dissolved oxygen

cues.title: Warm water — faster metabolism
lines: At 12 degrees — about 35 percent of optimum uptake
At 18 degrees — about 75 percent
At 22 degrees — 100 percent optimum
At 28 degrees — 120 percent but root rot risk rises

cues.title: Warming options ranked by effort
lines: Wait for warmer weather — free, +0
Insulate the reservoir — 5 to 15 euros, +2 to +4 degrees
Move tower to a sunny spot — free, +3 to +6 degrees
Aquarium heater 50 watt — 15 to 25 euros, +5 to +10 degrees

cues.title: Minimum kit — 20 euros
lines: Colombo Aquatest pH drop kit — 9 euros 99
Colombo Aquatest KH drop kit — 9 euros 99
Citric acid powder from Action — 2 to 4 euros

cues.title: Recommended kit — 35 to 40 euros
lines: Above plus a TDS or EC meter from Action or Bol — 10 to 15 euros
Cheap aquarium thermometer — 3 to 5 euros

cues.title: Stable-temperature kit — 60 to 80 euros
lines: Above plus Colombo Aquatest GH — 9 euros 99
Aquarium heater 50 watt with thermostat — 15 to 25 euros

cues.title: Fresh reservoir with rainwater
lines: 1 — Fill reservoir with rainwater
2 — Add A+B nutrients to EC 900 to 1200
3 — Stir, wait 10 minutes
4 — Test pH — expect 5.8 to 6.3
5 — Set pump duty cycle for stage
6 — Recheck every 2 to 3 days

cues.title: Fresh reservoir with tap water
lines: 1 — Fill with Almere tap water
2 — Add A+B to EC 900 to 1200 (deduct ~400 tap baseline)
3 — Stir, wait 10 minutes
4 — Test pH — expect 7.4 to 7.8 (high)
5 — Dose strong citric stock 1 mL at a time
6 — Continue until pH 6.0 — expect 5 to 10 mL total
7 — Recheck every 3 to 5 days
